statement.p: Factor the function formula g(x)=3x^2+3x-60 and use the result to identify the roots and the horizontal intercepts.

solution.p: The function formula factors as g(x)=3(x+5)(x-4)\text{.} So we can see that the roots are -5 and 4\text{.} These are the numbers that zero out the formula by canceling what we see in the formula. And the horizontal intercepts are (-5,0) and (4,0)\text{.}
